content.problemStatement:
There are NNN people participating in a race. The NthN^{th}Nth participant is your friend, so you want him to win. You are not a man of ethics, so you decided to inject some units of a Performance Enhancement Drug (don't ask where that came from) in your friend's body.


From the charts, you came to know the speed of every player. Formally, for a player iii, his speed is denoted by SiS_iSi​.
The change in speed with one unit of the drug is KKK units. Note that KKK can be negative, which means the drug has more side effects than benefits.
Of course, there will be a drug test before the race, so your friend will be caught if the number of units of the drug in his blood is greater than or equal to LLL.
You need to determine whether you can help your friend to win the race (with or without drugs), without getting caught.

**Note:** A player wins the race if he has the maximum speed among all the participants. If there are more than one people with a maximal speed, they tie at first place, and **no one** wins!


Input Format

First line will contain a single integer TTT, the number of test cases. Description of the test cases follows.
First line of each test case will contain three space-separated integers, NNN - the number of participants, KKK - the change in speed with one unit of the drug, and LLL - minimum units of the drug that can be detected in the drug test.
Second line of each test case will contain NNN space-separated integers SiS_iSi​, the speeds of the participants.


Output Format
For each test case print "Yes" if you can help your friend to win the race, otherwise "No" in a single line.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case: first line contains three integers N, K and L. The second line contains N space‑separated integers S1 … SN, the speeds of the participants. The N‑th participant (the last number) is your friend.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing "Yes" if you can make your friend the unique fastest runner without using L or more units of the drug, otherwise output "No".

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: friend speed 2, max other 3, K=2, L=2 → max allowed units =1, final speed =2+2=4>3 → Yes.\nTest case 2: friend speed 2, max other 4, final speed with 1 unit =4 (tie) → No.\nTest case 3: K is negative, friend cannot improve and is slower than participant with speed 12 → No.

hidden.label: positive K but L too small

solution.approach: For each test case compute the maximum speed among the first N‑1 participants (maxOther). Let friendSpeed = S[N]. If friendSpeed > maxOther the answer is "Yes" (no drug needed). Otherwise, if K ≤ 0 the drug cannot increase the speed, so answer is "No". If K > 0, the best we can do is to use the maximum allowed units, L‑1, because speed grows with each unit. The final speed would be friendSpeed + (L‑1)*K. If this value is strictly greater than maxOther, we can make the friend win, otherwise we cannot. The algorithm runs in O(N) per test case and O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T·N) – linear scan of the speeds for each test case.
